name: Croquettes Mexicano
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Manchego cheese, cut into 2-1/2 inch batons
recipeInstructions:
Note: See the "Chipotle Salsa" and "Crema" recipes which are included in this collection.
To make the dough, heat the lard over low heat in a small skillet. Saute the ancho chile for 1 to 2 minutes until slightly softened. Transfer the chile to a blender with peppercorns, salt, and water; blend into a smooth puree. In a large mixing bowl, combine the masa with the chile puree. Knead dough well. Test consistency by flattening a small ball of dough between palms; if edges crack, add water tablespoon at a time until no cracking occurs. Set aside covered at room temperature for 1 hour.
Form dough into football-shaped croquettes around cheese pieces (about 2-inch diameter). Heat oil in deep fryer or heavy skillet to 375°F. Fry croquettes, turning once golden, about 3 to 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels and transfer to warmed platter. Scatter shredded cabbage over top; sprinkle with lime juice. Serve with remaining limes and drizzle with Chipotle Salsa and Crema.
This recipe yields approximately 12-14 croquettes.
